session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the existing patterns for the following files that I'll be modifying. Please read each file and provide a summary of the key patterns:
1. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/schemas.py` - Look at PlanHeaderSchema, especially existing optional int fields like plan_comment_id 2. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/plan_header.py` - Look at existing extract/update functions for plan_comment_id 3. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/abc.py` - Look at existing method patterns (signatures, docstrings) 4. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py` - Look at how existing methods call gh CLI 5. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py` - Look at fake patterns 6. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/dry_run.py` - Look at dry_run delegate pattern 7. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/printing.py` - Look at printing delegate pattern 8. `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` - Look at PlanRowData 9. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/abc.py` - Look at fetch_ci_summaries signature 10. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py` - Look at fetch_ci_summaries and _build_row_data implementations 11. `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/fake.py` - Look at make_plan_row and fetch_ci_summaries 12. `src/erk/tui/screens/check_runs_screen.py` - Look at __init__ and how it calls fetch_ci_summaries 13. `src/erk/tui/actions/navigation.py` - Look at how CheckRunsScreen is instantiated 14. `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_generate_summaries.py` - The full file 15. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - Look at the ci-summarize job step
For each file, provide the complete relevant code sections I need to understand to implement the changes.

1→--- 2→title: PlanRowData Field Reference 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing command availability predicates" 5→ - "understanding what data is available for TUI commands" 6→ - "checking which PlanRowData fields are nullable" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-23 00:15 PT" 8→audit_result: edited 9→--- 10→ 11→# PlanRowData Field Reference 12→ 13→Quick reference for writing command availability predicates and understanding 14→`PlanRowData` usage patterns. 15→ 16→## Overview 17→ 18→`PlanRowData` is a frozen dataclass containing all data for a single plan row in the TUI. It combines raw data (for actions) with pre-formatted display strings (for table rendering). 19→ 20→**Location:** `src/erk/tui/data/types.py` 21→ 22→## Field Categories 23→ 24→### Plan Info 25→ 26→| Field | Type | Description | Nullable? | 27→| ------------ | ------------- | ----------------------------- | ----------------------------- | 28→| `plan_id` | `int` | Plan identifier | Never | 29→| `plan_url` | `str \| None` | Full URL to the plan | Yes | 30→| `full_title` | `str` | Complete plan title | Never (empty string possible) |
... [196 lines omitted] ...
80→| `run_conclusion` | `str \| None` | Run conclusion: "success", "failure", "cancelled" | Yes |

1→--- 2→title: CI Failure Summarization 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with CI failure summaries or ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers" 5→ - "modifying the ci-summarize workflow job" 6→ - "understanding how TUI displays CI failure details" 7→ - "debugging CI summary parsing or matching" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "changing the ci-summarize job `needs` array" 10→ warning: "The `needs` array must reference actual job names in ci.yml. Broken references silently skip the job. Verify every name exists." 11→ - action: "parsing ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers without re.DOTALL" 12→ warning: "Summary content is multiline. The regex uses re.DOTALL so `.` matches newlines. Without it, multiline summaries won't be captured." 13→ - action: "matching check names from summaries to GitHub check runs" 14→ warning: "GitHub prepends 'ci / ' to check names in statusCheckRollup. Use match_summary_to_check() which strips this prefix." 15→--- 16→ 17→# CI Failure Summarization 18→ 19→When CI checks fail on a PR, the `ci-summarize` workflow job generates human-readable summaries using Haiku. These summaries are embedded in the job's logs as structured markers, then parsed and displayed in the TUI. 20→ 21→## Architecture Overview 22→ 23→``` 24→CI job fails 25→ -> ci-summarize job runs (ci.yml) 26→ -> erk exec ci-generate-summaries fetches logs + calls Haiku 27→ -> Outputs ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers to stdout 28→ -> Markers stored in ci-summarize job logs 29→ -> erk exec ci-fetch-summaries retrieves + parses markers 30→ -> TUI check_runs_screen displays summaries as blockquotes
... [115 lines omitted] ...
80→5. Returns `dict[str, str]` (empty dict on any failure) 107→Individual job failures don't stop processing of other jobs. 121→- What specific check/test failed (file names, test names) 122→- The error message or root cause

1→--- 2→title: TUI Architecture Overview 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding TUI structure" 5→ - "implementing TUI components" 6→ - "working with TUI data providers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "reusing same DOM element id across loading/empty/content states" 9→ warning: "query_one() returns wrong element silently when id is reused across lifecycle phases. Use unique IDs per phase." 10→ - action: "calling widget methods directly from @work(thread=True) background threads" 11→ warning: "Direct widget calls from background threads cause silent UI corruption. Must use self.app.call_from_thread(callback, ...)." 12→ - action: "extending PlanDataProvider ABC" 13→ warning: "Requires 3-file update: abc.py + real.py + fake.py. Fake must initialize new dict in __init__. Missing init causes AttributeError at test time." 14→ - action: "adding a DataTable column with add_column(key=...)" 15→ warning: "Column key is a data binding contract — must match data field name. Silent failure when mismatched." 16→ - action: "moving @on decorated event handlers to a mixin" 17→ warning: "Textual's _MessagePumpMeta only scans class.__dict__, not inherited methods. Event handlers on mixins are silently ignored." 18→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 19→audit_result: clean 20→--- 21→ 22→# TUI Architecture Overview 23→ 24→The erk TUI is built with Textual and follows a layered architecture separating data fetching, filtering, and rendering. 25→ 26→## Directory Structure 27→ 28→``` 29→src/erk/tui/ 30→├── app.py # Main Textual App (ErkDashApp)
... [181 lines omitted] ...
126→Use fake providers instead of mocking. `FakePlanDataProvider` lives in `erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake` and accepts keyword-only arguments (`plans`, `clipboard`, `browser`, `repo_root`, `fetch_error`). The `make_plan_row()` helper in the same module creates test `PlanRowData` instances with sensible defaults.

1→"""Schema v2 plan header operations for erk-plan issues. 2→ 3→These support the new plan issue structure where: 4→- Issue body contains only compact metadata (for fast querying) 5→- First comment contains the plan content 6→- last_dispatched_run_id is stored in issue body 7→ 8→Optional fields (added over time, backward compatible): 9→- source_repo: For cross-repo plans, the repo where this plan will be 10→ implemented (e.g., "owner/impl-repo"). 190→ RuntimeError: If gh CLI command fails 242→ 2. Failed completed runs (failures are more actionable than successes)

1→"""Production implementation of GitHub operations. 2→ 3→Error Handling Philosophy: 4→ gh CLI invocations should let exceptions bubble by default. Callers are 5→ responsible for handling failures appropriately. Don't swallow exceptions 6→ and return None/False/default values - this masks real errors and makes 7→ debugging difficult. 8→ 9→ Legitimate "not found" cases (e.g., no PR exists for a branch) are handled 10→ by checking the response data, not by catching exceptions. 11→""" 12→ 13→import json 14→import logging 15→import secrets 16→import string 17→from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta 18→from pathlib import Path 19→from typing import Any 20→ 21→from erk_shared.debug import debug_log 22→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import GitHub 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.graphql_queries import ( 24→ ADD_REVIEW_THREAD_REPLY_MUTATION, 25→ GET_ISSUES_WITH_PR_LINKAGES_QUERY, 26→ GET_PR_CHECK_RUNS_QUERY, 27→ GET_PR_REVIEW_THREADS_QUERY, 28→ GET_WORKFLOW_RUNS_BY_NODE_IDS_QUERY, 29→ ISSUE_PR_LINKAGE_FRAGMENT, 30→ RESOLVE_REVIEW_THREAD_MUTATION,

1→"""Data types for TUI components.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→from dataclasses import dataclass 6→from datetime import datetime 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import IssueFilterState 9→ 10→ 11→@dataclass(frozen=True) 12→class PlanRowData: 13→ """Row data for displaying a plan in the TUI table. 14→ 15→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 16→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 17→ 18→ **Display vs Raw convention:** Many data points have both a raw field (for 19→ logic/predicates) and a display field (for table rendering). Raw fields are 20→ often nullable (None when absent), while display fields are always strings 21→ (dash "-" or empty when absent). Use raw fields in availability predicates 22→ (``ctx.row.pr_number is not None``), display fields for rendering. 23→ 24→ Attributes: 25→ 26→ plan_id: GitHub issue number (e.g., 123). Never None. 27→ plan_url: Full URL to the GitHub issue. None when unavailable. 28→ full_title: Complete untruncated plan title. Empty string possible. 29→ plan_body: Raw issue body text (markdown). Empty string possible. 30→

1→"""Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku. 2→ 3→Fetches failing jobs from a GitHub Actions run, retrieves their logs, 4→sends them to Claude Haiku for summarization, and outputs results 5→in ERK-CI-SUMMARY marker format (consumed by ci_summary_parsing.py). 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ erk exec ci-generate-summaries --run-id 12345 9→ 10→Output: 11→ ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers to stdout, progress messages to stderr 12→ 13→Exit Codes: 14→ 0: Success (even if individual jobs fail to summarize) 15→ 1: Error during execution (e.g., cannot fetch failing jobs) 16→ 17→Examples: 18→ $ erk exec ci-generate-summaries --run-id 12345 19→ === ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 20→ - Formatting issues in `src/foo.py` 21→ === /ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 22→""" 23→ 24→from __future__ import annotations 25→ 26→from dataclasses import dataclass 27→from pathlib import Path 28→ 29→import click 30→
... [166 lines omitted] ...
84→ f"Summarize this CI failure log for job '{job_name}' in 2-5 concise " 85→ f"bullet points. Focus on what failed and why.\n\n{log_content}" 98→ Individual job failures don't stop other jobs from being summarized. 108→ '.jobs[] | select(.conclusion == "failure") | "\\(.id)\\t\\(.name)"', 116→ click.echo(f"Error fetching failing jobs: {result.stderr}", err=True) 143→ click.echo("(Log fetch failed)") 166→ summary = "(Summarization failed)" 177→ """Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku.
session.assistant: Finally, let me get the CI workflow file snippet:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get the ci-summarize job from workflow

session.tool_result:
1→name: ci 2→ 3→on: 4→ push: 5→ branches: [master] 6→ pull_request: 7→ types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review] 8→ workflow_dispatch: 9→ 10→permissions: 11→ contents: write # Needed for autofix to push 12→ actions: write # Needed to re-trigger workflow after autofix push 13→ statuses: write # Needed for autofix to update commit status 14→ 15→concurrency: 16→ group: ci-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref }} 17→ cancel-in-progress: true 18→ 19→jobs: 20→ check-submission: 21→ if: github.event.pull_request.draft != true && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') 22→ runs-on: ubuntu-latest 23→ timeout-minutes: 5 24→ outputs: 25→ skip: ${{ steps.check.outputs.skip }} 26→ steps: 27→ - uses: actions/checkout@v4 28→ - name: Check for submission folder 29→ id: check 30→ uses: ./.github/actions/check-impl-context
... [75 lines omitted] ...
84→ echo "::error::Files need formatting or docs are out of sync" 93→ echo "::error::Formatting issues found (cannot auto-fix fork PRs)"

ci-summarize: needs: [ format, lint, fix-formatting, docs-check, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests, ] if: | vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false' && always() && (github.event_name == 'pull_request') && (github.event.pull_request.draft != true) && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') && (needs.format.result == 'failure' || needs.lint.result == 'failure' || needs.fix-formatting.result == 'failure' || needs.docs-check.result == 'failure' || needs.ty.result == 'failure' || needs.unit-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.integration-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.erk-mcp-tests.result == 'failure') runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 10 steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
... [1 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me get more of the ci-summarize job:
session.tool_result:
ci-summarize: needs: [ format, lint, fix-formatting, docs-check, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests, erk-mcp-tests, ] if: | vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false' && always() && (github.event_name == 'pull_request') && (github.event.pull_request.draft != true) && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') && (needs.format.result == 'failure' || needs.lint.result == 'failure' || needs.fix-formatting.result == 'failure' || needs.docs-check.result == 'failure' || needs.ty.result == 'failure' || needs.unit-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.integration-tests.result == 'failure' || needs.erk-mcp-tests.result == 'failure') runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 10 steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
